title: Patatas Bravas
description: A classic Spanish dish featuring crispy fried potatoes in a spicy tomato sauce, garnished with parsley and garlic.
instructions: Cut the potatoes into 1-inch cubes and place them in a bowl of cold water to remove excess starch.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Remove the potatoes from the water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Fry the potatoes in batches until they are golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
Drain the fried potatoes on paper towels and set them aside.
In the same pan, add the chopped garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red pepper flakes, if using, and cook for another minute.
Add the tomato sauce to the pan and stir to combine with the garlic and spices.
Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it has thickened slightly.
Add the fried potatoes to the sauce and stir to combine.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve the Patatas Bravas hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the Patatas Bravas rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
This dish is best served immediately, as the potatoes will absorb the sauce and become soggy if left to sit for too long.
Patatas Bravas can be made ahead of time and refrigerated or frozen for later use, but they are best served fresh.
To reheat the Patatas Bravas, heat them in a pan over low heat, adding a splash of olive oil if necessary to prevent the potatoes from sticking.
If reheating from frozen, thaw the Patatas Bravas overnight in the refrigerator and then reheat them as above.
